verdicts.mechanism: Lines 76–78 require `major >= 1` but apply `minor >= 21` only when `major != 1`, allowing urllib3 1.20 while rejecting 2.0.7.
At runtime TYPE_CHECKING is False, so is_prepared returns True even when request.url or request.method is None, unsafely narrowing an unprepared request to _ValidatedRequest.

For a MaxRetryError whose reason matches none of the raising branches—such as NewConnectionError—the `pass` at line 729 falls through with `resp` never assigned, so line 748 raises UnboundLocalError instead of ConnectionError.

Because the broad `except (_SSLError, _HTTPError)` immediately maps every non-`_SSLError` (including `ReadTimeoutError`) to `SSLError`, its later timeout/header branches are unreachable for those errors while actual `_SSLError` values fall through to raw re-raise.
Because TYPE_CHECKING is False at runtime and treated as True by type checkers, lines 18–21 perform Unpack/_t imports only at runtime while hiding them from static analysis.
For any 4xx response without WWW-Authenticate, line 291 assigns None to s_auth and line 293 unconditionally calls s_auth.lower(), raising AttributeError.

Because TYPE_CHECKING is False at runtime, line 48 selects the unconditional `import chardet` on line 49, so missing chardet raises ImportError and `_resolve_char_detection()` never tries `charset_normalizer`.
Line 87 calls self._r.headers.get(name) without passing default, so get_header("missing", "fallback") returns None instead of "fallback".
MockResponse.getheaders() calls self._headers.getheaders(name) without returning it, so every invocation implicitly returns None.
Line 516 evaluates `set(kwargs) + set(result)`, but sets do not support `+`, so every `create_cookie()` call raises `TypeError` before validation or cookie creation.
Although line 539 leaves expires=None, create_cookie always fails first at line 516 because Python sets do not support '+', so no session cookie is produced.
When response is None, the negated condition on line 33 evaluates true, so line 34 dereferences None as response.request and raises AttributeError.

When the pyOpenSSL import fails, OpenSSL is set to None at line 28, so `if not (OpenSSL)` is true and line 95 dereferences `None.__version__`, while a successful import makes the condition false and leaves version data unpopulated.
When a partial line sets pending at line 1025, the next iteration executes `pending - chunk` at line 1017, and subtraction is unsupported for both `str` and `bytes`, raising TypeError.

At lines 1074–1075, `if not (self.encoding is None)` runs only when an encoding is already set and overwrites it with `self.apparent_encoding`, while leaving `encoding` as `None` when auto-detection is actually needed.
Line 19 enters the aliasing block only when `mod` is neither `target` nor a `target.*` submodule, so the intended chardet-compatible modules are skipped while unrelated loaded modules are aliased.
Line 324 enters deletion unless both Authorization exists and should_strip_auth is true, causing KeyError when absent and retaining credentials when cross-host stripping is required.
Line 787 adds the post-send clock reading to `start`, so every completed `send()` assigns an inflated `r.elapsed` instead of the actual duration obtained by subtraction.
At module import, _init() executes line 122 and subtracts one str from another via (__doc__ + "\n") - "\n".join(...), which raises TypeError.

Lines 55–57 replace every non-None data argument with {} and pass None unchanged to MutableMapping.update, causing construction both to discard supplied entries and to raise TypeError when data is omitted.
LookupDict.get accepts default but line 130 calls self.__dict__.get(key) without it, so any missing key returns None instead of the caller-supplied default.

The `except BaseException` around `yield tmp_handler` deletes the temporary file and then executes `pass`, suppressing write or replacement exceptions and making the context manager exit successfully without updating the target.
Line 564 slices from idx-1, so `charset=utf-8` stores `charset: t=utf-8`, and line 584 returns that malformed encoding.
